skill.name: amazon-listing-competitor-analysis-skill
skill.description: Analyze one Amazon competitor listing by ASIN using BrowserAct extraction, then summarize listing strengths, keyword patterns, review themes, market gaps, and strategic opportunities.
skill.summary: Analyze Amazon competitor listings and turn extracted listing data into market opportunity insights.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, environment variable access, and an agent-invoked Python script. Review found no prompt injection, obfuscation, malicious exfiltration, or real weak cryptography, but the skill sends a BrowserAct API key and listing inputs to a third-party API.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Access With Environment API Key
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a Bearer token to https://api.browseract.com. This is intended for BrowserAct, but it exposes a secret to a third-party service and requires user trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential source and outbound Authorization header are clear in the code. The behavior appears legitimate for BrowserAct use, so confidence is high but not critical.

medium_findings.title: External Listing Data Processing
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script sends the ASIN and marketplace URL to BrowserAct and polls external task endpoints. This is core functionality, but users should know listing inputs and task results leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP calls and payload fields are explicit. The risk is contextual because the network use matches the skill description.

low_findings.title: Agent-Invoked Python Script
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python script with an ASIN and optional marketplace URL. The example quotes arguments and the script does not execute shell commands internally, limiting command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command is part of the documented invocation path. No evidence found of shell interpolation or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static detections in SKILL.md mostly refer to markdown inline code, example command formatting, and output template text. No evidence found that these backticks execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows documentation syntax and a fenced output format, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. This is a strong false positive.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported SKILL.md lines contain prose, parameter names, or output-template text. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing, ciphers, or security-sensitive algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no crypto operations. The scanner likely matched short substrings inside normal listing-analysis vocabulary.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Benign Status Handling
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported lines handle API errors, status polling, and skill feature descriptions. No evidence found of host reconnaissance, system probing, or local environment enumeration beyond checking one required API key.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are normal operational guidance and API response checks. There is no code reading hostnames, processes, networks, or local system inventory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script combines an environment API key with outbound requests to BrowserAct. This is expected behavior, but it is the main security-sensitive pattern users must understand.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header is constructed directly from the API key and used in each requests call. No evidence found of additional secret collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Critical Combination Heuristic
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The network, credential, and script patterns are present, but review found no obfuscation, hidden exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious control flow. The combination supports the stated BrowserAct workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination exists structurally, but all reviewed behavior maps to documented API extraction. No evidence found of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Amazon teams need fast competitor research without manually reviewing every listing detail. This skill extracts listing data and turns it into grounded SEO, review, content, and opportunity insights.
actual_capabilities: Runs a BrowserAct workflow for one Amazon ASIN.
Extracts visible listing fields, images, seller data, and review-related blocks.
Maps keyword placement across title, bullets, description, and A plus content.
Summarizes competitor strengths in content, visuals, and buyer proof.
Identifies market gaps and strategic opportunity points for the user.
Handles invalid API keys, concurrency limits, and one retry for transient failures.
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key in the environment.
Sends listing inputs and task data to BrowserAct.
Analyzes one ASIN at a time.
Depends on Amazon page availability and BrowserAct workflow results.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Launch Gap Review
use_cases.description: Compare a planned product position against one strong competitor and find gaps to own.

use_cases.title: Audit Keyword Placement
use_cases.description: See where a competing listing places primary, secondary, and long-tail search terms.

use_cases.title: Mine Buyer Feedback
use_cases.description: Use reviews to find praised benefits, objections, unmet needs, and offer opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ASIN Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze ASIN B0CS62LY6P on Amazon.com. Summarize what the listing does well and the main gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword Strategy Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this competitor ASIN for keyword placement. Separate title, bullets, description, and A plus content insights.

prompt_templates.title: Review-Led Opportunity Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this ASIN and focus on review themes. Identify buyer-valued benefits, complaints, and product opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Market Competitive Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this ASIN on the German Amazon marketplace. Compare content, keywords, visuals, and strategic openings for my launch plan.

output_examples.input: Analyze a competitor ASIN for a new product launch.
output_examples.output: A competitor teardown with listing strengths, keyword placement, buyer-validated proof, market gaps, and opportunity points.

output_examples.input: Find review themes that my brand can address.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of praised benefits, recurring complaints, unmet buyer needs, and offer ideas grounded in extracted reviews.
A visual strategy readout covering image sequence, infographic claims, missing proof types, and creative differentiation options.

best_practices: Use one competitor ASIN with the correct regional Amazon marketplace URL.
Review extracted facts before making strategy decisions.
Treat competitor data as evidence for your positioning, not as copy to reuse.
anti_patterns: Do not analyze private or restricted data without permission.
Do not paste API keys into chat when environment variables are available.
Do not turn competitor output into direct copied listing text.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to run the BrowserAct extraction workflow.

faq.question: Can it analyze multiple ASINs at once?
faq.answer: No. The included workflow is designed for one ASIN per run.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from my browser?
faq.answer: No. It sends the task to BrowserAct, which performs the extraction workflow.

faq.question: Can I use Claude, Codex, or Claude Code with it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Will it create a new Amazon listing for me?
faq.answer: No. It produces competitor intelligence and opportunity points, not final listing copy.

faq.question: What should I verify before using the results?
faq.answer: Verify extracted listing facts, regional marketplace context, and any business claims before acting on them.
